job_summary: Child FIRST-Family Services Practitioner
job_ad_details:
About Us:

At Anglicare Victoria our focus is on transforming the futures of children and young people, families and adults. Our work is based on three guiding pillars, Prevent, Protect, Empower. We offer a comprehensive network of services and seek to ensure the provision of high quality services that will bring about significant improvements in the life experience of the young people, children and families/caregivers with whom we work.

The Position:

Child FIRST-Family Services aims to support parents to ensure the social, emotional, educational, health and developmental outcomes for their children. In addition, the Child FIRST-Family Services program aims to increase the safety of children and young people and prevent families entering the child protection system.

The Outer East Child FIRST - Family Services program responds to need in the Knox, Maroondah and Yarra Ranges local government areas. Anglicare Victoria has Child FIRST and Family Services teams working across Lilydale and Bayswater sites. Child FIRST-Family Services Practitioners;

  • Receive referrals into Child FIRST program, provide information and advice to referrers and families and engage vulnerable families in need.
  • Undertake needs and risk assessments on children, young people and families using the Best Interest Case Practice Model.
  • Work flexibly and collaboratively with Child Protection, the Aboriginal Liaison Worker and other agencies to conduct thorough assessments and determining an appropriate service response.

To Be Successful in this Position You Will Need:

  1. A relevant tertiary qualification in Social Work, Psychology, Early Childhood Specialist and/or related behavioural sciences at degree level with substantial experience; or associate diploma level with substantial experience in the relevant service stream, or less formal qualifications with specialised skills sufficient to perform at this level.
  2. Demonstrated professional resilience in working with and supporting clients who have been exposed to trauma, violence or neglect.
  3. Demonstrated ability to use a range of active engagement strategies with vulnerable and at risk children, young people and their families, and be flexible and creative in meeting their needs.
  4. An understanding of the social context of child abuse and neglect, and experience in using current theoretical approaches to support positive outcomes.
  5. Sound understanding of the Child Protection and welfare system, and experience in collaboration with a range of professionals in a care team approach.
  6. Experience in conducting risk and needs assessments.
  7. Excellent written and verbal communication, time management and organisational skills.
  8. Ability to advocate, negotiate and problem solve.
  9. Understanding of the community’s cultural diversity and ability to practice with cultural competence and safety.
  10. Ability to partake in reflective practice and to be self-directed.

Please Note: It is mandatory for the successful applicant to hold a current Working with Children Check and be prepared to undergo a National Criminal History Check prior to commencement.

For a detailed Position Description PD-Child FIRST-Eastern.pdf

job_ad_details:
About Us

At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. 

With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities.  Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success. 

Come and be a part of this success.

About the Role

We have an opportunity for a Project Engineer to join our Modifications & Projects team. The team delivers projects, each typically up to a maximum USD$10M in value, across the Nelson Point and Finucane Island port facilities. The projects are multi-discipline in nature. Reporting to the Lead Project Engineer your responsibilities will include:
  • Providing input into the Modifications and Small Projects portfolio and the related budgets, plans and schedules.
  • Where required, supporting feasibility studies, cost-benefits analysis, impact assessments and project business cases to obtain project approvals.
  • Project management of the allocated Projects from initiation to handover to scope, budget, schedule, standards, and specifications in accordance with relevant processes and BHP Small Project Management Framework.
  • Regular project reporting and status updates.
  • Producing the specified key project deliverables, using internal and internal resources, to the cost, schedule and quality requirements required for the particular project..
  • An overriding commitment to health, safety, environment responsibility and sustainable development including supporting safe execution of the works and undertaking quality field leadership activities
  • Cultivating positive relationships through effective engagement, communication and consultation.

About You

Drawing on your previous experience in delivering small/medium Projects (value up to USD$10M), and ideally a formal qualification (Grad Cert, Diploma or similar) in Project Management you will have a solid understanding of the full-cycle of Project Management. Your natural confidence and exceptional interpersonal skills will enable you to build relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders, which will be key in communicating progress and ensuring project alignment to stakeholder requirements. Strong aptitude in commercial and contractor management will be highly regarded as you will be required to negotiate with both internal and external stakeholders. A tertiary qualification in Electrical, Mechanical, or Civil engineering would be highly advantageous but not mandatory. Alternatively a strong trade background/ construction experience would also be well regarded for this position. 

A C-Class driver’s license will be a mandatory requirement for this position.

Location 
You will have the option in this role of being either FIFO 5/2 4/3 (flexible rosters available) from Perth or Residential in Port Hedland. 

Residential:
In this residential role you will be based in the coastal town of Port Hedland, Western Australia. Port Hedland is home to our port and rail operations and is the largest regional centre in North West Australia.  The 20,000-strong population enjoys well-established education services, entertainment, shopping and medical facilities. 

The excellent sporting and leisure facilities include; a new aquatic centre, sports stadium and water play park. Its beaches and mangroves are home to an abundance of fish and other wildlife.  Within driving distance are the spectacular Broome, Karratha and Karijini national Park. As this is a residential position you will also be provided with subsidised rental accommodation in addition to power and water subsidies.

FIFO:
You will be based at Port Hedland on a fly-in fly-out 5/2 4/3 roster.  Port Hedland is home to our port and rail operations and is the largest regional centre in North West Australia.   The outstanding facilities at the FIFO villages include; a fully equipped gym, TV lounge and games rooms. Additionally you will have access to the state of the art aquatic and sporting facilities in the town of Port Hedland. All rooms at the villages have their own en-suite bathroom, television, refrigerator and air-conditioning. 

Remuneration and Benefits

Join us and be rewarded with interesting, challenging work in a supportive team culture. Develop skills for life whilst enjoying access to benefits including; incentive bonus, award winning share scheme, education assistance and generous superannuation scheme. As part of our commitment to inclusion and diversity, we offer a range of family friendly work practices, including flexible work arrangements, and a substantial parental leave policy, includes retirement savings / superannuation contributions during parental leave.

Supporting a Diverse Workforce

At BHP, we recognise that we are strengthened by diversity. We are committed to providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and we encourage applications from women and Indigenous people.

We know there are many aspects of our employees’ lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life.  These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.

The Emergency Services Telecommunications Authority (ESTA) provides the critical link between the Victorian community and the State's emergency services agencies for police, fire, ambulance and Victoria State Emergency Service.  ESTA manages the provision of advanced, operational communications and provides Victoria's 24-hour triple zero ‘000' emergency call-taking and dispatch services.   ESTA's operational communications service is provided across three sites, with the goal for each facility to host emergency communications for all emergency services (police, fire abulance & SES).

The Program Manager will be responsible for ensuring the delivery of the above projects.

The variety and challenge of this role will come from:

  • Develop all key project artefacts including the development of a detailed Project Management Plan, risk log, issue log and change register, schedule and budget

  • Deliver all project all procurement and contract activities to meet the needs of ESTA and the projects.
  • Monitor, assess and report on overall progress of the project against cost, time and scope tolerances, quality, risk and benefits criteria.

  • Identify and assess all issues and risks for the project and prepare recommendations.] for review and decisions
  • To foster good staff relations both internally within ESTA and externally with ESOs, vendors and other project stakeholders.

You will have:

  • Demonstrated project management experience in complex facilities/accommodation projects, including site move projects and management of a small team
  • Strong stakeholder engagement skills with a focus on exceeding customer expectations and the ability to influence and obtain support
  • Demonstrated experience in the transition management of large mission critical systems.
  • Demonstrated experience in facilitating technical testing activities and device roll-out/logistical activities across large geographical areas.
  • A well-organised, independent approach with ability to handle pressure and deal with a wide range of stakeholders and conflicting views. Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation skills

  • Exposure or qualification to agile/lean methodologies
  • Demonstrated experience in the public sector (probity, audit, public records, etc.), preferred.

This role will be responsible for the coordination of labour coverage to a designated operational area to maximise operational effectiveness, as well as maintain roster maintenance.

You will ensure the right number of appropriately skilled staff are allocated to the right tasks at the right time via workforce planning systems.

The Role

Reporting to the Ground Operations Workforce Planning Manager, key responsibilities of this role are:

  • Administer staff/roster movements and the allocation of labour on the day of operation.
  • Coordinate staffing levels through changes in operation demand and labour supply
  • Liaise with operational managers on a daily basis to coordinate daily staffing levels.

  • Demonstrated ability to effectively work under day to day operational pressures
  • Positive contributor to the team environment which needs to adapt to the changing needs and priorities of the business.
  • A keen understanding and overall knowledge of airport operations
  • High level of computer literacy skills with the ability to adapt to new IT systems
  • Technical knowledge of the Groundstar systems (desirable)


About Us

The Jetstar Group has airlines in Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, Japan and Vietnam. Since launch in 2004, we have grown from a team of 700 to 7,600 and have flown more than 250 million passengers. Collectively we now offer more than 5000 flights a week to more than 85 destinations in 17 countries and territories across the Asia Pacific region, with a fleet of more than 130 aircraft.

Our Culture
Jetstar is alive with a sense of adventure. Day in and day out, our teams search for new places to fly, new ways to add value and new ideas to improve performance. If you love the road less travelled, you’ll love working here because ours is a team who innovate every day, thrive on delivery, support each other and celebrate our successes. You’ll be supported to challenge, anticipate and explore while you work will connect people and enrich lives. We encourage diversity and aim to create an environment where our employees feel valued, respected and enabled.

Our Benefits
Team members have access to heavily discounted fares to destinations all over the world – not only for team members, but for their favourite travel companions too. Our staff travel benefit includes discounts on airfares, accommodation, car hire and travel insurance for both domestic and international travel. Discounts can be used within the Jetstar Group airlines, Qantas and other partner airlines.

job_summary: Utilise your clinical skills by providing medical support for some of SA's most iconic events. Staff a medical post and respond to emergencies.
job_ad_details:
About the business

Event Medical Plus is a South Australian organisation that is focused on providing the community with professional medical and healthcare services.

Our account managers have a collective experience in excess of 50 years in the area of event medical services and have had direct involvement in many of South Australia's significant and iconic events and venues. Using this experience, your dedicated Account Manager will tailor solutions that meet your needs in the most cost-effective way possible.

Established in 2014, Event Medical Plus raises the standard in service delivery and clinical care.

About the role

Due to a period of growth, we are now seeking a number of healthcare practitioners enter our casual pool, and as such be eligible for various casual shifts that are available from time to time, in the capacity of 'Paramedic'. It is the role of a Paramedic to provide emergency medical assistance to injured or ill persons in collaboration with other Event Medical Plus and non-Event Medical Plus Healthcare Practitioners or services, in accordance with the Event Medical Plus Clinical Framework at events, where Event Medical Plus services are contracted and Healthcare Practitioners are rostered. Healthcare Practitioners support to the deployment, execution and withdrawal of Event Medical Plus services, equipment and equipment to an event.

Event Medical Plus Healthcare Practitioners are selected based on:

  • Personal and professional presentation;
  • Relevance of clinical and non-clinical experience;
  • Satisfactory outcome of pre-employment checks, including a current DHS or DCSI Working with Children Check, Pre-Employment Medical Assessment (where requested) and Reference Checks, as well as the provision of satisfactory evidence in accordance with the respective clinical accreditation level listed below.

For initial accreditation, must provide evidence of:

  • Current, full registration with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulatory Authority ('AHPRA'), as a Paramedic; and
  • Eligibility for employment by SA Ambulance Service or the state ambulance authority in their state or territory of residence; and
  • Holding a Paramedic Authority to Practice; or
  • Active involvement in an ongoing professional Clinical Development Programme; and
  • Hold a current approved Advanced Life Support Level 1 Qualification (Australian Resuscitation Council or Australian College of Critical Care Nurses); and
  • Completion of relevant EMP Induction.

For ongoing accreditation, must provide evidence of:

  • Current, full registration with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulatory Authority ('AHPRA'), as a Paramedic; and
  • Eligibility for employment by SA Ambulance Service or the state ambulance authority in their state or territory of residence; and
  • Holding a Paramedic Authority to Practice; or
  • Active involvement in an ongoing professional Clinical Development Programme; and
  • Completion of relevant Event Medical Plus Induction; and
  • Satisfactory result from a DHS  Working with Children Check; and
  • Completion of any upgrade packages of new, modified or revised clinical guidelines, standing orders or equipment guides.

Benefits and perks

Our Healthcare Practitioners must be able to demonstrate the ability to continue to maintain clinical competency. For this reason, we encourage our staff to work in other clinical settings and use the widely varied and exciting work with us to broaden their clinical experience and supplement their income, with competitive remuneration rates.

Skills and experience

Paramedic

Our paramedics are qualified to a level that is equivalent to that of a practising paramedic with the state ambulance authority. As of December 1st 2018, they are registered as a 'Paramedic' with the Paramedicine Board of Australia. Event Medical Plus paramedics are either currently practising as state ambulance authority paramedics, or are healthcare professionals at an equivalent level to state ambulance authority paramedics.

job_summary: We are looking for candidates with a tertiary biological science qualification to join our team
job_ad_details:
Laboratory Technician

Full time - Kenepuru Science Centre

Institute of Environmental Science and Research Ltd (ESR)

About ESR

ESR is a Crown Research Institute, working with and for clients and stakeholders in central and local government, industry organisations and the private sector to deliver enhanced scientific research services for the economic, environmental and social wellbeing of New Zealand. ESR has approximately 400 staff with sites in Auckland, Christchurch, Upper Hutt, and Porirua.

About the role

We are seeking an enthusiastic person with a positive attitude and microbiology laboratory experience, to help manage the New Zealand Reference Microbiology Culture Collection- Medical Section (NZRM). This interesting and varied role will include planning their own work schedule to keep the NZRM catalogue up to date, maintain stocks of freeze dried culture, purity checking, maintain accreditation, client liaison, and invoicing. Other duties will include general microbiology and molecular typing methods in our Kenepuru Science Centre reference laboratories.

A tertiary biological science qualification is a requirement to gain the necessary registration with the Medical Sciences Council of New Zealand. job_summary: Lead the day-to-day coordination of the Centre of Research Excellence for the Development of Innovative Therapies for Psychiatric Disorders (CREDIT)
job_ad_details:
School of Medicine/ Centre of Research Excellence for the Development of Innovative Therapies for Psychiatric Disorders (CREDIT)

 The core vision of CREDIT is to advance a national strategy and platform supporting the development and translation of novel therapies for the most prevalent and disabling neuropsychiatric disorders, in particular depression, bipolar disorder and schizophrenia.

  • Located Geelong
  • Part-time (18.375 hours per week)
  • Fixed Term 12-month Contract

The Role

The CREDIT Officer will conduct activities that support the Centre of Research Excellence for the Development of Innovative Therapies for Psychiatric Disorders (CREDIT). The incumbent will be required to lead the day-to-day administration, management, and coordination of the newly-instituted Centre. The Officer will work closely with the Director, Executive, and the CREDIT Investigator Committee. This is a new fixed-term part-time position funded by grants from the NHMRC Centres of Research Excellence (CRE).

To thrive in the role you will have research experience and a passion for working in a new, highly innovative and coordinated approach to treatment discovery in mental health care in Australia. With ability to build and maintain effective client relationships across the University, particularly with the CREDIT Investigator Committee and other researchers within the School of Medicine. The confidence to liaise with media, industry partners, professional societies, and politicians to represent and promote CREDIT.

To be successful, you will have;

  • completion of a degree in a related field with subsequent relevant work experience, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training.
  • project management expertise and a track record of delivering projects within specified timelines and budgets.
  • high-level administrative skills, including efficiently managing budgets and financial activities, event coordination, and proficient use of the Microsoft Office Suite.
  • demonstrated ability to develop collaborative work teams with a dedicated focus on achieving skilled, functional teams.
  • the ability to research, interpret and apply up-to-date information to develop training materials.
  • solid working knowledge and/or hands-on experience in website development.

Primary Responsibilities

  • manage the day-to-day logistical and administrative processes that ensure the smooth running of the CREDIT Investigator Committee, including organising and attending relevant meetings.
  • work collaboratively with the Director and Executive in developing and maintaining excellent strategic and research partnerships with key stakeholders connected to CREDIT.
  • implement and manage a grant improvement program to provide high-level support to early career researchers (ECRs) in submitting grant applications.
  • implement and manage a website to publicise information about CREDIT to the general public and research and clinical communities.

Culture

Deakin University is proud to be recognised as an organisation that offers a friendly, supportive and challenging working environment. Our staff are committed to making a genuine difference to people’s lives through excellence in education and research.

We acknowledge the importance of providing a dynamic and diverse working environment and offer variety in day-to-day roles as well as professional development opportunities to assist staff to grow and progress their careers.
Deakin University staff have the opportunity to interact with colleagues from a diverse range of cultures and professional backgrounds, all of whom share a common interest in lifelong learning.

Benefits

We value our staff and understand that they are more than just their jobs, that's why we offer benefits that enhance work life. Some of Deakin’s workplace benefits include:

  • 17% superannuation
  • flexible work arrangements
  • discounts with third party suppliers for health insurance, car rentals IT and more

About us

The flexible learning options we provide our students and our diverse team of professionals and academic experts has enabled us to achieve a 5-star rating by the prestigious university ranking organisation Quacquarelli Symonds. For eight consecutive years, we've achieved the highest level of overall student satisfaction among Victorian universities.

We have a network of campuses, learning centres and research centres across Victoria and corporate offices in Melbourne, China, Indonesia, India and Sri Lanka.  Our online Cloud campus provides more than 13,000 students with access to state-of-the-art digital tools and direct access to world-renowned academics.

As a Victorian university with a global impact, Deakin is translating its research into commercial outcomes that will help drive innovation for Australia’s economy.

Please submit your updated resume and a short cover letter matching your skills and experience for this role.

Position Description:  PD- CREDIT Officer.pdf

At Deakin we value diversity, embrace difference and nurture a connected, safe and respectful community. Deakin is an Employer of choice for Gender Equality, a proud member of the SAGE Athena SWAN program seeking gender equity for Women in STEMM, and a bronze award holder in the Australian Workplace Equality Index for LGBTI inclusion.  We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people of all cultures, abilities, sex and genders.

With research in nutrition and growth, respiratory disease, allied health, environmental health, burns, infectious diseases, cerebral palsy, rehabilitation and child and youth mental health and more, the centre is making a difference to the health of children everywhere.

The UQ-CHRC is co-located with research centres from Children’s Health Queensland and Queensland University of Technology (QUT) in the recently completed Centre for Children’s Health Research CCHR), a $134 million purpose-built research facility adjacent to the Queensland Children’s Hospital. Information relating to the activities of CHRC may be found at https://child-healthresearch.centre.uq.edu.au/.

About This Opportunity

This is a unique opportunity for an experienced Clinical Trials Assistant to conduct work within the UQ Child Health Research Centre (UQ-CHRC) that brings together some of UQ’s leading researchers to work on global challenges in child and adolescent health.  In particular, this opportunity will work closely with Research Nurses, Principal Investigators and a broader clinical trial team in the infants and children intensive care unit.

The role will focus on conducting clinical trials, including but not limited to; obtaining consent from families of various clinical studies, data entry into clinical databases, and general day-to-day administrative support.

Our Ideal Candidate

This role requires a well presented, empathetic and mature person with the following skills and experience: 

  • Undergraduate degree in science, health, or related discipline, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training
  • Experience as a Clinical Trials Assistant (or similar) within a clinical research setting.
  • Demonstrated experience in consenting patients or their families with a sensitive and mature approach within a clinical based environment.
  • High level of interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to deal with issues of a sensitive nature.
  • Demonstrated ability to be highly organised and collaborative to support a gamut of projects across the project team.
  • Independent thinking with the ability to be proactive and work autonomously when required.
  • Experience in data entry into clinical databases uses a High level of attention to detail and accuracy. Familiarity with research data entry systems such as REDCAP is desirable.
  • You must have ongoing unrestricted work rights in Australia to apply for this opportunity

We value diversity and inclusion, and actively encourage applications from those who bring diversity to the University. Our Diversity and Inclusion webpage contains further information if you require additional support. Accessibility requirements and/or adjustments can be directed to recruitment@uq.edu.au.

What We Can Offer

This is a full-time, fixed term position for 12 months at HEW level 5. The full-time equivalent base salary will be in the range $67,608 - $75,506, plus super of up to 9.5%.

For further information, please review The University of Queensland's Enterprise Bargaining Agreement 2018-2021.

Position Description

507975_ Clinical Trials Assistant.pdf

It is a condition of employment for this role that if you are required now or in the future, to work or interact in Queensland Health clinical facility; or in an equivalent clinical health facility; or health care role; or will be required to perform work tasks that put you at risk of exposure to vaccine-preventable disease you are required to be immunised against, and remain immunised against, certain vaccine preventable diseases (VPDs) in accordance with the University’s Vaccinations and Immunisation Guidelines (PPL 2.60.08).  The employee is required to provide evidence of immunisation against VPDs.

job_summary: Metro Trains is looking for an Occupations Coordinator to join Rail Infrastructure Alliance working on the Metro Tunnel Project.
job_ad_details:
Company description:

Are you interested in joining Metro Trains' Metro Tunnel Team and bringing your wealth of Occupations/ Possessions experience to support the delivery of Victoria's biggest rail infrastructure project?

If the answer is "yes", keep reading!

Metro Trains Melbourne (MTM), is a proud partner in the delivery of major rail infrastructure projects across Melbourne, including the Metro Tunnel Project. The Metro Tunnel Project will deliver more frequent services for passengers by unlocking the City Loop and introducing new technology and infrastructure that will transform our network.

About the project:
The Metro Tunnel Project (MTP) is being delivered in three work packages, with a program level team assisting each package. The three work packages are: Rail Systems Alliance (RSA), Rail Infrastructure Alliance (RIA) and the Tunnels and Stations (T&S) Public Private Partnership. This role will be working as part of the Rail Infrastructure Alliance based at Macaulay Road but will be working in between different sites depending on the Occupations.

Job description:

About the role:
You will be responsible for providing effective coordination of all Occupations and associated works in relation to the RIA. The core responsibilities of role will include:

• Assist in the management of the Works Readiness process and ensure that T minus Stage Gates are achieved, including the completion of Works Readiness Checklists and submit them on or ahead of time for each RTO
• Coordinate with each RTO Network Safety and Operations teams to ensure that all Occupations and their subsequent Completion of Works Readiness Checklists as required
• Ensuring the timely submission of Occupations within ARMS as required
• Co-ordinate with Network Safety and Operations to ensure that all Occupations and their subsequent limits are correctly represented and submitted within ARMS
• Manage the preparation and development of Draft Protection Diagrams for endorsement by Network Safety
• Manage and contribute to the preparation and publishing of key Occupation documentation such as Works Guides
• Plan and/or attend Occupation co-ordination meetings across all Project Packages to identify potential synergies
• Convene post-Occupation evaluation meetings (Lessons Learned) in continuing to improve the Planning process and to ensure that any corrective measures are highlighted and implemented
• Liaise with other Rail Transport Operators, where Works are proposed at or across jurisdictional boundaries

The role requires weekend and Public Holiday working onsite to deliver Occupations (usually 1 weekend per fortnight and shuts as required) and a project allowance will be paid to cover these periods.

About your experience:

• Current Track Force Protection Coordinator - Level 3 (TFPC-3) qualifications, Safeworking experience
• Understanding and ability to produce Protection Diagrams
• Experience with the booking and implementation of critical resources across all RTOs

About the opportunity:

This is a fantastic opportunity to build on the career you've developed to date and take it to the next level by making a tangible impact that matters. If what you've read resonates with your values, experience and career aspirations then we want to hear from you!

Why work for Metro?
• Progressive organisation with a ‘One Team, One Metro' mantra
• 2-yr max term Full Time role working on the Metro Tunnel Project
• Free public transport throughout Victoria

Metro Trains is an equal opportunity employer and embraces diversity. We encourage all interested applicants to apply but please note that only applications with the right to live and work in Australia will be considered for this position.

job_summary: WorkSafe Victoria has entered into an exciting period of transformation to better serve the community we take pride in keeping safe
job_ad_details:
• Executive Officer Position
  • Fixed Term Opportunity to mid-March 2020
  • Geelong Location

About the role

Our Intelligence Management Function (IMF) is a centralised data and analytics division which harnesses the use of data and analytics to generate insights, make better decisions and promote a more prevention-led and client-centric approach.  We are uplifting our tools and techniques which will help us to better understand our clients and their needs predict risk and prevent harm in the workplace.

As our Chief Data & Analytics Officer, you will continue to drive the amazing work currently developed within the function which will keep the momentum driving forward as we continue to enhance our business.


A key driver of success in this role will be your ability to successfully engage, influence and inspire not only the team but the wider WorkSafe business. Effective and collaborative partnering with the business will be key in order to define, foster and embed a culture of a customer centric approach and decision making based on data and analytics insights across the business.

Key accountabilities for this role include but are not limited to:

  • Continuing to deliver the Data and Analytics operating model, including the ongoing development of analytics team structure, and enhancing our new way of working
  • Designing and implementing the Data Governance Framework on an enterprise level to ensure that important data assets are formally managed, and that trusted information is used for critical business processes, reporting and decision making
  • Effective and collaborative partnering with the business in order to add clarity, accountability and insight to business performance and improvement initiatives
  • Guiding strategic thinking to use data and analytics to continue to drive better decisions for WorkSafe’s operation
  • Engagement of our Senior Executive Leadership team on progress and enhancements to our operating rhythm and progress in aligning to WorkSafe 2030 strategy

Please ensure you take a look at the Position Description provided for additional information on accountabilities for this role. www.wemake.worksafe.vic.gov.au

About you

You are an experienced Chief Data & Analytics professional who wants to make a difference. You are experienced in driving the ongoing performance and strategies in place within a business which keeps the momentum of the businesses progress. Your exceptional communication and organisational skills are imperative in engaging our senior stakeholder and executive leadership team. As this is a new function which kicked off on 1st April 2019, you are experienced with building a culture of team work, supportive environment and leading the teams for success and empowerment.

  • Experience managing analytical teams to deliver strategic and operational objectives, including through a heightened period of change
  • Substantial experience in analysing, advising and reporting on complex analytics in order to influence the ongoing development and success of the business
  • Significant experience in implementing data governance and quality control
  • Strong ability to prepare business papers with the ability to analyse complex information and to develop a relevant and robust business case
  • Relevant post graduate qualifications and/or extensive relevant experience (background in statistics and mathematics preferred but not necessary

Working with Us

We pride ourselves on being a great place to work and offer flexibility to facilitate work/life balance. People who are seeking part-time or job share arrangements are encouraged to apply.

WorkSafe employees enjoy: Discounted Gym & Pool Memberships | WELL Rated Building | Group Training & Yoga Classes | Very active Social Club | Pool & Table Tennis | Sparkling Water | Discounted Prescription Eyewear | Career Opportunities and Working for your Community!

What’s next?

Applications close 11:00pm on Tuesday 18 June 2019. Click the link to apply, and join us in making a difference to Victorian lives.

Only people with the right to work in Australia may apply for this position. You may be required to undergo a National Police Check (and Medical Assessment) as part of the recruitment process.

Candidate screening may take place prior to the advertisement closing, so get your application in.

For further enquiries please contact Keiran Jones on 03 9641 1040.

About Us

WorkSafe has a proud history. Over more than 30 years, we have worked hard with employers and workers to create safer workplaces.

During this time, Victoria’s workers’ compensation scheme has helped more than one million injured workers receive compensation, rehabilitation and medical services, while maintaining one of the lowest premium rates in Australia.

However, our workplaces and industries are changing rapidly and we are rapidly changing to be more prevention lead and client centric to our community.

Our future

Our goals are to embed in every workplace a proactive, prevention-led approach to health and safety across the state, and to provide a personalised, tailored service for every Victorian who comes into contact with WorkSafe.

Like wearing a seatbelt or putting on sunscreen, “prevention-led” thinking will become automatic every time Victorians step into the workplace.

WorkSafe are striving to build a diverse workforce that reflects the Victorian community we serve.

job_summary: THE GPT GROUP Listed on the Australian Securities Exchange since 1971 the GPT Group is today one of Australia's largest diversified listed...
job_ad_details:
THE GPT GROUP
Listed on the Australian Securities Exchange since 1971 the GPT Group is today one of Australia's largest diversified listed property groups. The Group has a substantial investor base, is one of the top 50 stocks by market capitalisation and has $19 billion of assets under management.

GPT's goal is to be Australia's best performing property company, through our focus on strategic investment and management of high quality shopping centres, office towers, logistics centres and business parks.

Core to GPT's culture are our values which include Authenticity, Courage, Future Shaping and Accountability.  Our focus on  innovation allows the business to find new opportunities and identify the trends that will shape the future of the sector.

THE ROLE

As the Senior Research Analyst/Research Manager, the successful candidate will be responsible for conducting strategic investment and customer insights analysis to advise the GPT Group Retail Business including the Retail Division, GPT Wholesale Shopping Centre Fund, Capital Transaction and Retail/Mixed Use Development Team.

 

This role is responsible for:

 

  • Working with Head of Retail Research to formulate and execute strategic analysis.
  • Developing and managing research program and requirements for GPT assets and GPT Retail Business.
  • Build strong working relationships with retail team, investment team, asset management team, leasing and retail/mixed use development. 
  • Assisting to identify and manage research conducted by third party consumer/economic consultants.
  • Proactively respond to request from all business units in a timely manner.
  • Delivering retail houseviews to Head of Retail Research and assist in communicating final outputs to Head of Investment, Head of Asset Management and Fund Manager.
  • Delivering business strategy packs for retail business planning.
  • Assisting with delivering value-add pieces to the business based on current/future retail trends.
  • Assisting with presentations to senior leaders within the business.

 

To be considered for this role you will need:
 

  • Degree qualified.
  • At least 5+ years’ experience and proven track record in financial or data analysis.
  • Interest in consumer behaviour, retail and property.
  • Proactive self-starter with ability to manage multiple projects.
  • Excellent analytical skills with ability to draw actionable insights.
  • Enjoy new challenges, problem solving and a fast learner.
  • Advanced excel, PowerPoint and word skills.
  • Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills.
  • Attention to detail, achievement orientated, hardworking and proactive.
  • Tableau / SQL / Basic Programming desirable but not require.

At GPT, we recognise that providing our people with flexible working options improves our performance, creates opportunities for learning, supports wellbeing and improves our lives outside of work. Our mobile workplace technologies and activity based work environments make working at GPT what you do, not where you do it. Explore your options to achieve flexibility including: generous paid parental leave, tailored hours and flexible scheduling, career breaks, study leave, compressed hours, remote working and leave purchase plans.

GPT is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All suitably qualified applicants will receive equal and fair consideration for employment. 

  ASX listed property company Experience in consumer behaviour analysis is desirable Competitive remuneration package

SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

ABOUT YOU

  • Highly organised and enjoy being part of a talented and creative team
  • Must take ownership of an issue through to resolution

SMX OFFERS YOU

A highly competitive salary package including healthcare, ongoing development, and potential offshore conferences. You will work with a highly experienced and respected team. job_summary: Excellent opportunity to join ResMed who provide innovative products that improve the lives of millions of people with sleep and respiratory disorders
job_ad_details:
Materials Purchasing Co-Ordinator

Sydney

At ResMed (NYSE: RMD, ASX: RMD) we pioneer innovative solutions that treat and keep people out of the hospital, empowering them to live healthier, higher-quality lives. Our cloud-connected medical devices transform care for people with sleep apnea, COPD and other chronic diseases. Our comprehensive out-of-hospital software platforms support the professionals and caregivers who help people stay healthy in the home or care setting of their choice. By enabling better care, we improve quality of life, reduce the impact of chronic disease and lower costs for consumers and healthcare systems in more than 120 countries. To learn more, visit ResMed.com and follow @ResMed.

The Operations team partners closely with ResMed’s five business leaders to understand their needs then providing a variety of products and services to help them meet those needs including Customer and Technical Services, Global Quality Assurance and Regulatory Affairs, Supply Chain, manufacturing, logistics and distribution. Focused on enabling new product introduction and operating excellence, our team strives to improve time to market for new products and services, improve efficiencies in our cross-functional processes, and improve our supply chain through digitization and automation to increase value add to our customers.

Let’s talk about the team and you:

This is an excellent opportunity to join ResMed on a permanent basis. This role will be located at Bella Vista, Sydney and will report to the Materials Controller within Operations, Logistics.

Let's talk about Responsibilities:

  • To assist with the day-to-day material control functions for the Sydney production site in ordering and expediting materials and ensuring Oracle system is up-to-date continuously with data related to material flow.
  • This function works closely with our Global Supplier Alliance, Logistics and Production teams to optimise material flow from suppliers, through the manufacturing processes, to availability for shipping to the regions as finished products, in a timely manner.
  • Growth means an increasing reliance on best practise inventory control systems to keep timely yet minimum materials at each stage of the supply chain.
  • The global environment is an additional challenge to this role, requiring awareness of changing inventories and demands around the world. Efficient and timely communications of issues in manufacturing, and responsiveness to changing demands are critical to the success of this function.

Let’s talk Qualifications and Experience:

To be successful in this role, you will have tertiary qualifications with at least 3 years of experience in a similar role. In addition you will have strong numeric skills, high attention to detail and accuracy in a fast paced environment. An understanding of logistics and supply chain will ensure you are a strong candidate for this position as will experience using an ERP system such as Oracle.

Okay, so what’s next?

Joining ResMed is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. Our hope is that each day you’ll uncover a new reason to love what you do. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now!

Please note unsolicited resumes from employment agencies or other third parties will not be considered. ResMed is an Equal Opportunity Employer and all applicants must be legally entitled to work in the country the role is based out of.
